rules.title: Reading paper has 42 scored items
rules.body: An authored Reading paper has exactly 42 one-point scored items: Part A has 20, Part B has 6, and Part C has 16.

rules.title: Part topology
rules.body: Part A contains 4 medical texts on a single topic, Part B contains 6 short workplace extracts, and Part C contains 2 long healthcare articles.

rules.title: Question types by part
rules.body: Part A may use matching text reference, short answer, or sentence completion items. Part B uses exactly three-option multiple choice. Part C uses exactly four-option multiple choice.

rules.title: Canonical scoring anchor
rules.body: Reading raw score 30 out of 42 is exactly 350 out of 500. AI authoring must not produce learner grades or replace canonical OetScoring.

rules.title: Human approval is mandatory
rules.body: AI-extracted Reading structures are draft content only until an admin reviews and explicitly approves them. There is no production auto-approval path.

rules.title: Do not invent answer keys
rules.body: AI extraction may only use the supplied question-paper and answer-key text. Missing answers, passages, options, or explanations must remain missing/invalid rather than guessed.
